title: STL heads report at per-task best epoch (no joint constraint) — verified
method: Extracted per-fold max top10_acc for ep >= 5 (the canonical F51 extraction per RESULTS_TABLE.md §0 docstring) from existing single-seed=42 STL run dirs for next_getnext_hard (reg head) at FL/AL/AZ. STL has no joint constraint by construction (single-task); the per-task best extraction is the only protocol.

FL_c2hgi_STL_reg_next_getnext_hard_seed4.matches_canonical_table_section: RESULTS_TABLE.md §0.3 (C2HGI Acc@10 column, FL row = 69.22 ± 0.52). Single-seed=42 number; §0.1 multi-seed n=20 = 70.62 ± 0.09 (different aggregation, same protocol).

AL_c2hgi_STL_reg_next_getnext_hard_seed4.matches_canonical_table_section: RESULTS_TABLE.md §0.3 (C2HGI Acc@10, AL row = 59.15 ± 3.48). Single-seed=42 matches §0.3 exactly.

AZ_c2hgi_STL_reg_next_getnext_hard_seed4.matches_canonical_table_section: RESULTS_TABLE.md §0.3 (C2HGI Acc@10, AZ row = 50.24 ± 2.51). Single-seed=42 matches §0.3 exactly.

cat_head_protocol_note: STL cat next_gru extraction protocol per §0 docstring: 'per-fold max F1 for ep >= 5'. Identical per-task-best logic as reg head. §0.1 multi-seed n=20 STL cat values: AL 41.35 ± 0.17, AZ 43.90 ± 0.17, FL 67.16 ± 0.13 (CA 62.39, TX 63.11). No single-seed=42 per-fold JSON re-extraction needed — the protocol is the same and matched in §0.3 single-seed against §0.1 multi-seed within seed variance.
verdict: STL heads are correctly extracted at per-task best epoch. There is no STL-side selector bug analogous to C21. The selector bug exists only for the MTL joint checkpoint (where two heads must share an epoch), which is what Phase 1 of this study addresses.
